select_when: A complex problem needs to be decomposed into manageable branches before the team can solve and recombine it coherently.
Workstreams are proliferating and the next risk is incoherent synthesis unless the merge rule is made explicit.
A high-stakes recommendation needs both rigorous analysis and a clear integration path back to one decision.
The team must move from scattered issue work toward a structured whole without losing the causal logic of each branch.
avoid_when: Teams are branching aggressively without any credible merge rule, so more decomposition would only increase fragmentation.
The problem is genuinely novel and a familiar branch frame is being imposed too mechanically for comfort or speed.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on decomposition, bounded workstream solving, and explicit merge discipline rather than on consulting jargon alone.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor process sprawl because the raw source is stronger on branch discipline, merge coherence, and anti-brittle-framework warnings.
open_questions: The output type compresses a source that also spans high-stakes communication and issue-tree based analysis.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit on decomposition, solve-path rigor, and merge discipline for complex strategic decisions.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions are grounded in source warnings on merge-less branching and brittle reuse of familiar frameworks on novel problems.

input_type.note: The source clearly activates on multi-branch problems that need recombination, while the typed input is a compact reviewed label.

output_type.note: The source yields an integrated answer after branching and solving; the typed output compresses that result into an operational artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Branch-solve-merge is strongest as systems-aware causal structuring, while the taxonomy labels remain normalized.
